module_map.responsibility: Request/response pipeline core: BaseHandler orchestrates view dispatch; WSGIHandler/ASGIHandler adapt to servers; exception handling and middleware registry
HTTP abstractions: HttpRequest/HttpResponse envelope data structures; content negotiation and media handling
URL routing and resolution: URLResolver routes requests to callbacks; RegexPattern/URLPattern define route patterns; ResolverMatch provides matched route info
ORM core: Model base class; field systems; querysets and database operations; model instantiation and saving lifecycle
Database backend abstraction and query execution: connection management; cursor handling; transaction control; SQL compilation
Middleware registry and common security/functional middleware: CSRF protection; clickjacking protection; XSS handling
Contrib apps infrastructure and reusable components: auth, admin, sessions, messages, staticfiles, etc.; extensible via django.contrib.contenttypes framework
Application registry and configuration: AppConfig lifecycle; application loading; ready() callbacks
Settings and configuration system: Settings module; LazyObject patterns; configuration defaults and validation
Template engine and rendering: Engine registry; context processors; template loading and caching
Utilities and polyfills: timezone, crypto, text utilities, cache, versioning, module_loading

pipeline_trace.description: Raw WSGI/ASGI environ parsed into HttpRequest object; encoding determined; content-type/accept negotiation; request metadata exposed via request attributes (META, GET, POST, FILES)
URLResolver matches request.path_info against pattern database; resolves to view function/callback; locale handling for i18n; raises 404 if no match; populates resolver_match on request

BaseHandler.get_response wraps request in middleware layers; middleware process_request chain invoked in reverse order; view function called with request and kwargs from resolver_match; exceptions captured/re-raised
View instances QuerySet instances and database access; ORM compiles queries and delegates to backend; produces Model instances or Cursor; models are descriptors defining field machinery

View returns HttpResponse or subclass (JsonResponse, StreamingHttpResponse); BaseHandler wraps response in middleware process_response chain; content-type, status, headers finalized; debug tooling mutates body on exceptions

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central pipeline orchestrator: BaseHandler mediates between HTTP layer (request parsing), URL routing, middleware, exceptions, and ORM layer. It maintains RequestNode, processes request/response middleware chains, and dispatches exceptions to registered exception handlers.

Middleware registry and envelope: Middleware classes act as bridges that transform request/response across layers. They import heavily from http (request/response), register under MIDDLEWARE settings (conf), and plug into handlers. CSRF/XFrame middleware exemplify cross-layer coordination.

ORM as data backbone: Models are central abstractions consumed by views, forms, admin, and serialization; they generate queries executed by db.backends; signal emissions; app registry integration. Serves as critical data exchange layer between presentation and persistence.

change_proposal.feature_summary: Add async-compatible structured logging middleware that captures per-request metadata (view name, ORM query count, response time, status) and emits structured logs. Logs should support correlation IDs for async tasks and include trace context.
new_types: {"type": "LogRecordContext", "description": "Context holder for per-request structured logging metadata (request_id, view_name, start_time, query_count, traceparent)"}

{"type": "LogMiddleware", "description": "Async-compatible middleware that initializes LogRecordContext, emits start log on request, updates query count during ORM hooks, and emits end log on response with elapsed time and status. Follows middleware process_request/process_response signature.".
{"type": "QueryCounter", "description": "Thread-local/async-local counter to track number of ORM queries executed per request; incremented via signal or proxy that hooks ORM execution.".
{"type": "TraceParent", "description": "Structured trace context following W3C Trace Context; enables request correlation across async boundaries and services."}
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Middleware design follows existing middleware classes like CsrfViewMiddleware and XFrameOptionsMiddleware: define process_request/process_response hooks; store state on request object or middleware instance; respect middleware ordering via ordering attribute; integrate with app registry and __init__.py exports; follow Django’s lazy settings pattern for configuration options.
change_proposal.integration_point: Middleware integrated into MIDDLEWARE setting in conf/global_settings.py and per-project settings. Registered after SecurityMiddleware and before CommonMiddleware. Incorporates QueryCounter via signal hooks on django.db.backends.signals; emits logs via logging.info with structlog/json formatter configured via LOGGING dict in settings.
risks: Performance overhead: Structured logging (JSON serialization, I/O syscalls) can degrade response times in high-throughput apps; mitigated via async logging handlers and rate-limiting/buffering. Legacy adapters for loguru/structlog may add complexity.

Backwards compatibility: Introducing new middleware changes the request object by attaching new attributes; must avoid naming collisions and provide clean adaptation layer. Existing middleware depending on request timing may break if logs delay response finalization.

Async compatibility: Django’s async views and middleware require careful handling of async locals for request-scoped context and logging coroutines; improper context management may leak memory or produce duplicate records across async calls.

tool_usage.params: ?/django/http/request.py extract HttpRequest class and key methods for pipeline I/O
?/django/urls/resolvers.py extract URL resolution mappings and ResolverMatch for routing stage
?/django/core/handlers/wsgi.py extract WSGIHandler workflow and BaseHandler lifecycle
?/django/db/models/base.py extract Model class and base ORM lifecycle methods
?/django/middleware/csrf.py extract CsrfViewMiddleware for middleware pattern and process methods
?/django HttpRequest symbol tracing for usage across test suite
